decision: PM run 2026-06-02 v13: (1) Pre-flight complete; develop HEAD 6fdb6e97 (v11 PM chore). (2) GitHub: 3 open PRs — #452 (release/v0.1.17→main, founder-gated; Quality Gate ✅ but publish-crates ❌), #453 (back-merge), #454 (PM chore v12, 20/20 CI ✅). 0 open issues. (3) Merged PR #454 (PM dispatch v12 chore, squash). (4) Diagnosed PR #452 `publish to crates.io` FAILURE: wait_for_crate loop used only 12×10s=120s; crates.io REST-API visibility lag exceeded that window after real publish. finalize was SKIPPED (downstream dep on failed publish) so main was not touched — no Charter §5.12 violation. Identified governance risk: with RELEASE_BOT_TOKEN configured, a successful publish would have triggered auto main-merge. (5) Opened PR #455 (ci/fix-release-yml-crates-wait-and-finalize-gate): (a) wait 12→36 attempts/360s, (b) finalize gated on workflow_dispatch. (6) Updated PM state v13 + dispatch table.
rationale: PR #454 was cleanly green and safe to merge. The release.yml failure on PR #452 was a timing/tooling issue, not a code quality issue — Quality Gate (required check) is SUCCESS. The governance risk from RELEASE_BOT_TOKEN + unconditional finalize job warranted an immediate CI fix on develop. The v0.1.17 ceremony should proceed via ceremony script without waiting for PR #455 to apply to the existing release branch.

artifacts.next_action: founder: run ceremony script for v0.1.17; PM/rust-implementer: merge PR#455 when CI green
